hero.headline: Solving complex problems through simple solutions.
hero.body: A personal blog and portfolio tracking my growth across robotics, embedded firmware, AI, and the practical discipline of making hardware behave in the real world.
hero.caption: A living archive of decisions, experiments, notes, and systems that moved from bench tests to field-ready behavior.
hero.imageAlt: Abstract robotics lab visualization with circuit traces and telemetry paths

focus.title: Applying AI and ML in Robotics
focus.copy: Using AI and ML to gain a tactical advantage and make decision-making easier.

focus.title: Custom Robotics Frameworks
focus.copy: A robust, lightweight stack deployable on 8-bit, 16-bit, and 32-bit architectures.

focus.title: Autonomy Tooling
focus.copy: Designing mission CLIs, route definitions, calibration files, and vision alignment loops.

focus.title: System Reliability
focus.copy: Documenting wiring, pinouts, sensor states, and failure modes so field testing becomes repeatable.

principles.title: Make the test visible
principles.copy: Every tool should expose enough state to understand what the hardware is doing and why.

principles.title: Prefer repeatable motion
principles.copy: A clean demo matters less than a motion sequence that can be replayed, inspected, and debugged.

principles.title: Document the interface
principles.copy: Pinouts, packet structures, calibration values, and command references are part of the product.

principles.title: Keep learning close to building
principles.copy: The fastest growth has come from closing the loop between theory, code, wiring, and field behavior.

contact.heading: Let’s build something precise and useful.
contact.copy: I’m interested in robotics, AI/ML, embedded systems, and perception systems that make engineering decisions easier to trust.
